LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT THAI HOUSE EXPRESS' MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT, AS TO PUNITIVE DAMAGES ALLEGATIONS FROM THE COMPLAINT FILED BY PLAINTIFF NICHOLAS LINDSEY IS GRANTED. NO OPPOSITION FILED. THE COURT SIGNED THE PROPOSED ORDER SUBMITTED WITH THE MOVING PAPERS. JUDGE: HAROLD E. KAHN; CLERK: G. GONZALES; REPORTER: REYES F. HUNTER CSR#6576, EMAIL: RHUNTER@SFTC.ORG.

calendar.matters:
CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Nov-05-2018 Off Calendar

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Aug-02-2018 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Nov-20-18 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Feb-22-2018 Set For Trial On Sep-17-18 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-11-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance.

RULING: There being no appearance or objection, the Court adopts the tentative ruling.
DEFENDANT THAI HOUSE EXPRESS' MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT, as to punitive damages allegations from the complaint filed by plaintiff Nicholas Lindsey is GRANTED. No opposition filed.

The Court signed the proposed order submitted with the moving papers. Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Clerk: G. Gonzales; Reporter: Reyes F. Hunter CSR#6576, email: rhunter@sftc.org.
